license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ksrcwd6
title: Assign taxonomy to gene calls using Centrifuge
author: James E Thornton Jr

steps_raw.instruction: Log into the HPC $ ssh hpc$ ocelote $ ssh hpc $ ocelote
Move into your class directory. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username

Clone the Centrifuge github repository into your class directory on the HPC. $ pwd/rsgrps/bh_class/username$ git clone git@github.com:jetjr/Centrifuge.git $ pwd /rsgrps/bh_class/username $ git clone git@github.com:jetjr/Centrifuge.git
Move into the Centrifuge directory. $ cd Centrifuge
Edit the config.sh file to include the correct variable declarations. The following steps will detail how the config.sh file should be edited. $ nano config.sh
export CENT_DB="/rsgrps/bh_class/b_compressed+h+v/b_compressed+h+v"

export FASTA_DIR='/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes' FASTA_DIR should point to the directory containing your nucleotides.fna file generated from step 2 and transfered to the anvio-genes directory.
export TYPE="single"
export FILE_EXT='faa'
export REPORT_DIR='/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/taxonomy/' The program will create this directory for you. Make sure to replace username.
export PLOT_OUT='/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/taxonomy/' Same as REPORT_DIR but make sure to include the trailing / as stated in the config.sh file.

These should be named according to what sample your working with. For example, ocean data may name these: export PLOT_FILE='ocean_depth' export PLOT_TITLE='ocean_depth' PLOT FILE will be the file name of the bubble plot that is generated. PLOT TITLE will be the title found on the actual plot.
export FILE_TYPE="f" The nucleotides.fna file is in FASTA format.
The exclude parameter can be left blank. export EXCLUDE=""
Save and quit config.sh
Submit the job using the submit script found in the Centrifuge directory. $ ./submit.sh
Status of the job can be determined by the following command: $ stat -u username

This program uses R packages that must be installed prior to launching the job. Load the R module. $ module load unsupported$ module load markb/R/3.1.1 $ module load unsupported $ module load markb/R/3.1.1
Launch R. $ R
Get the "optparse" package. >

Get ggplot2 and plyr packages. You may be prompted to select a mirror. Any US server will work. > install.packages( install.packages("plyr")" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="BC185DB59C8345958F546B21287C3D65" > > install.packages("ggplot2") > install.packages("plyr") If you receive an error when installing the dependencies, continue with the protocol.
Quit the R session. Do not save workspace image. > q()> Save workspace image? [y/n/c]: n > q() > Save workspace image? [y/n/c]: n
Navigate to the directory on your local machine that contains the contigs.db generated during the Anvi'o protocol .

Extract gene calls from the contigs database. $ anvi-get-dna-sequences-for-gene-calls -c CONTIGS.db -o nucleotides.faa Important: nucleotides.fna was generated in the prodigal protocol. HOWEVER, we will be using this version from Anvi'o for taxonomy assignment. Remember windows users you must launch Anvio using docker. docker run --rm -v /path/to/files:/my_data -p 8080:8080 -it meren/anvio:latest
Make an anvio-genes directory. $ mkdir anvio-genes

On your local machine, scp the nucleotides.fna file generated from step 2 into the newly created anvio-genes directory. $ scp nucleotides.fna username@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes
A successful job will generate a centrifuge_report.tsv file in anvio-genes/taxonomy.
Move into the script directory. $ cd scripts
Edit the PBS variables in centrifuge_single_tax.sh to include the bh_class group and your email. #PBS -W group_list=bh_class #PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu $ nano centrifuge_single_tax.sh
Save and quite centrifuge_single_tax.sh. Then move back into the main Centrifuge directory. $ cd ..
